title: Micranthes × geum
taxonomy: Micranthes × geum is a hybrid species of flowering plant within the saxifrage family (Saxifragaceae). It is a cross between the two species Micranthes oregana and Micranthes aprica. This hybrid is commonly known for its distinctive flowers and foliage. As a hybrid, Micranthes × geum is not considered a distinct species but rather a product of plant breeding. The classification of Micranthes × geum reflects its complex genetic makeup and unique characteristics.

intro: Micranthes × geum, also known as a hybrid of the Alpine Bittercress and the Yellow Saxifrage, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Micranthes × geum include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
